summary: Iter35 frontend review — /operate verdict + sticky-tabs treatment verified end-to-end. 34/35 Playwright checks pass. Demo flow renders OPTIMIZATION VERDICT (LIFT · 100% confidence · +€15,156 · Quick Wins · reason: 'Listing scores avg 35/100. Fix title + photos + pricing — €15k/yr unlocked in week one.'). All 7 sticky tabs present (overview, snapshot, revenue, listing, redesign, yield-plan, action-plan) with sticky position:sticky/top:64px. Verdict bg correctly tinted emerald rgba(92,122,78,0.1) for LIFT. All 6 sections render. Back-to-landing works (URL stays /operate). Cross-shell isolation confirmed — /invest still renders bronze-on-dark landing.
critical_code_review_comments: OperateTabsBar IntersectionObserver: same caveat as InvestTabsBar (iter34) — rootMargin '-30% 0px -55% 0px' with threshold 0.25 occasionally misses sections shorter than the 15% trigger band, so the active tab may not flip to 'redesign' immediately after a programmatic smooth-scroll click. In manual user scrolling it tracks correctly (verified: snapshot becomes active on scroll-up). Consider threshold 0 with pure rootMargin trigger zone, OR set active tab synchronously inside the scroll(id) handler before relying on the observer to take over. Not a blocker — visual experience is fine in practice.

Verdict palette: LIFT, REPRICE, REDESIGN colors (#5C7A4E, #A07242, #9B6B40) are visually close to each other on parchment. Cross-page A/B with /invest's bronze verdict feels distinct as required, but REPRICE vs REDESIGN tinting could be confusable if both ever appeared on the same screen. Architecturally fine since they're mutually exclusive per asset.

OperateVerdict.jsx receives monthly_uplift_eur prop but does not consume it (uses verdict.optimized_monthly_rev_eur and verdict.projected_annual_uplift_eur). Either remove the unused prop or wire it for the 'monthly delta' line for consistency with iter34's parity model.
test_credentials: Public demo routes — no auth needed.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing /operate demo via operate-demo-btn.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter35 mirrors iter34's verdict + tabs treatment from /invest onto /operate using parchment+emerald palette. Demo (Athens 1-bed, ADR=118, occ=67, default refresh state) yields verdict=LIFT, strategy='Quick Wins', uplift=+€15,156 — matches spec. All testids present and correct: operate-section-verdict, operate-verdict-confidence, operate-verdict-badge, operate-verdict-reason, operate-verdict-uplift, operate-verdict-strategy, operate-tabs-bar, operate-tab-{overview,snapshot,revenue,listing,redesign,yield-plan,action-plan}. Skipped backend regression per E1 instruction (no backend changes). Cross-shell isolation visually confirmed: /invest = bronze-on-dark, /operate = emerald-on-parchment.
rca of the issue: N/A — no blocking issues. The 1 'failed' check (redesign tab emerald border after click+smooth-scroll) is an IntersectionObserver geometry edge case, not a defect: the observer's trigger zone (15% slice at viewport vertical center) didn't intersect the redesign section after the scroll(top - 110) offset settled. When user scrolls naturally, observer tracks correctly (verified by snapshot tab gaining emerald border on scroll-up). Mitigation suggestion: have scroll(id) optimistically setActive(id) before letting the observer take over, identical to many production sticky-nav implementations.
